input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**
```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `IsRefreshing` (bool) | Controls refresh spinner |
| `RefreshCommand` (IAsyncRelayCommand) | Calls API, sets `IsRefreshing = false` when done |
| `SelectedItem` (Item?) | Tracks selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` (IRelayCommand) | Navigates & resets `SelectedItem = null` |

**Key ViewModel logic:**
```csharp
[RelayCommand]
async Task Refresh()
{
    var data = await _apiService.GetItemsAsync();
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
    IsRefreshing = false;
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task SelectionChanged()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"detail?id={SelectedItem.Id}");
    SelectedItem = null; // clear so user can re-select same item
}
```

**Tips:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the same item can be tapped again.
- `IsRefreshing = false` must be set explicitly to dismiss the spinner.
- Use `SelectionMode="Single"` (not `Multiple`) to get tap-to-navigate behavior.

This is a knowledge/guidance question, not a code modification task. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
